## What is eDNA?

**Environmental DNA (eDNA)**

> *"Any DNA collected from an environmental sample without first isolating targeted organisms"*  
> — Taberlet 2012

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="70%"}

- Environments: water, soil, sediment, air
- Free DNA, particle-bound DNA, organelles, cells, tissue
- From viruses to fish, crustaceans, and plants
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
[🌊🌱🍃]{style="font-size: 50px;"}
:::
::::

<hr>

Most common eDNA data types you may encounter:

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Environmental DNA Metabarcoding**
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Quantitative PCR (PCR)**
:::
::::

::: notes
Environmental DNA is any DNA collected from an environmental sample without first isolating target organisms (Taberlet 2012). The most common we'll likely encounter as OBIS nodes is metabarcoding and qPCR. For this workshop we'll focus more on metabarcoding
:::
